title: Grist
description: Open-source modern relational spreadsheet that combines the flexibility of a spreadsheet with the robustness of a database.
installation: Grist stores each document as a SQLite .grist file on disk under /persist/docs, so the persistent volume is mandatory and holds all your user data. The bundled PostgreSQL add-on is used only for the Grist 'home' database (orgs, workspaces, users, sharing/ACL metadata) via the TYPEORM_* variables; rotate the Postgres password. Rotate GRIST_SESSION_SECRET to a fresh random value (openssl rand -hex 32) before going live, as it signs session cookies. APP_HOME_URL is wired to the public URL token; keep it correct or document sharing links and the API will break. By default this image runs with anonymous/no-login access (GRIST_DEFAULT_EMAIL is set as the initial admin) which is fine for a trusted single-tenant install; to require login configure an external auth provider (GRIST_FORCE_LOGIN plus SAML/OIDC env vars) or front it with an authenticating proxy. Sandboxing uses gVisor by default and falls back gracefully; no extra service required. No external object store is needed for a single-node deployment.
